figures.caption: Figure 6: Normalized regret as a function of policy iteration, across team sizes K ∈ {2, 4, 6, 8, 10} (rows) and non-stationarity levels ρ ∈ {0, 1, 2} (columns). Each curve averages over 300 seeds and the five values of λint; shaded regions show standard errors. CAPO, MA-GRPO, and HA-GRPO run for T = 25(K + 2) iterations; C3 runs for T = 25 iterations.
Figure 8: Regret versus cumulative real-environment calls for CAPO (orange) and CAPO-Direct (green) at λint = 0.25.
Figure 7: Regret versus cumulative real-environment calls for CAPO (orange) and CAPO-Direct (green) at λint = 0 (purely additive reward).

Figure 3: CAPO (orange) versus the direct-effect-only ablation CAPO-Direct (green): AUC regret as a function of the non-stationarity parameter ρ, across team sizesK ∈ {2, 4, 8} (rows) and interaction strengths λint ∈ {0, 0.25, 0.5, 0.75, 1.0} (columns). Each point averages over 300 seeds; error bars are standard errors. CAPO-Direct is at least as good at ρ = 0; CAPO pulls ahead cleanly by ρ = 10 and widens further at ρ = 20.
Figure 4: Per-agent advantage MSE at K = 16, broken down by agent index.
Figure 9: Regret versus cumulative real-environment calls for CAPO (orange) and CAPO-Direct (green) at λint = 0.5.
Figure 10: Regret versus cumulative real-environment calls for CAPO (orange) and CAPO-Direct (green) at λint = 0.75.
Figure 11: Regret versus cumulative real-environment calls for CAPO (orange) and CAPO-Direct (green) at λint = 1.0 (aggregate pairwise-interaction variance matched to additive variance).

Figure 5: Per-cell normalized regret AUC as a function of interaction strength λint, across team sizes K ∈ {2, 4, 6, 8, 10} (rows) and non-stationarity levels ρ ∈ {0, 1, 2} (columns). Each curve averages over 300 seeds; bars show standard errors.
